# Requirements Quality Checklist: MCP Tooling and Test Runbook

**Purpose**: Unit tests for requirement quality covering MCP tool contracts, runbook requirements, edge cases, and traceability
**Created**: 2026-03-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)
**Depth**: Standard PR review
**Audience/Timing**: Reviewer during implementation and PR validation
**Scope**: MCP configuration, health/auth/CRUD contracts, required `npx` flow, manual SQL verification instructions

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Are all mandatory tool families explicitly specified (health, auth, CRUD) rather than implied? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003..FR-007]
- [X] CHK002 Are required response fields defined for every tool category, not just examples? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-008]
- [X] CHK003 Are setup requirements for both MCP configuration and database connectivity fully documented? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002]
- [X] CHK004 Are testing-document obligations complete across setup, inspector usage, and manual SQL verification? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015]
- [X] CHK005 Are all in-scope CRUD tables explicitly enumerated and bounded? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007]

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK006 Is canonical configuration naming unambiguous (`WB-Workflow-Configuration.yaml`) with no conflicting alternates? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001]
- [X] CHK007 Are `user_logon` outcome distinctions (`SUCCESS`, `DENIED`, `ERROR`) clearly defined by condition? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005]
- [X] CHK008 Is the meaning of `status_message` sufficiently defined for objective reviewer interpretation? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008]
- [X] CHK009 Are manual SQL verification instructions specified at requirement level rather than left to implementation discretion? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015]
- [X] CHK010 Is the required `npx` command requirement explicit enough to avoid substitute-tool ambiguity? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012]

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK011 Do runbook requirements in functional requirements align with quickstart contract expectations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011..FR-015]
- [X] CHK012 Are table-scope constraints consistent across user stories, requirements, and assumptions? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK013 Are auth mock constraints consistent with non-goals that exclude production security behavior? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK014 Do architecture constraints remain consistent with MCP-only persistence access requirements? [Consistency, Spec §Constitutional Constraints]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK015 Are success criteria measurable without requiring undocumented tooling or hidden environment assumptions? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001..SC-006]
- [X] CHK016 Is “under 15 minutes setup” tied to a reproducible procedure and clear start/stop boundaries? [Acceptance Criteria, Ambiguity, Spec §SC-003]
- [X] CHK017 Is manual direct-database verification success objectively testable from requirements wording? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-006]

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK018 Are primary scenarios specified for config load, health checks, auth, and CRUD flows? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1..3]
- [X] CHK019 Are alternate scenarios specified for optional PostgreSQL verification without weakening required SQLite path? [Coverage, Spec §FR-015]
- [X] CHK020 Are exception scenarios defined for malformed config, DB outage, and invalid payloads? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK021 Are recovery expectations defined after failure states (e.g., reconnect or corrected input retry path)? [Coverage, Gap]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK022 Are unknown-table and missing-primary-key behaviors specified with clear required outcomes? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-009]
- [X] CHK023 Are invalid pagination constraints clearly specified for `list_[table]s` requirements? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-010]
- [X] CHK024 Is the “inspector unavailable” scenario covered with required fallback/documented reviewer path? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK025 Are traceability requirements for external-source/prompt updates explicitly mapped to this feature’s outputs? [Non-Functional, Traceability, Spec §Constitutional Constraints]
- [X] CHK026 Are documentation discoverability requirements specific enough for first-time reviewers? [Non-Functional, Measurability, Spec §SC-003, Spec §FR-011]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK027 Are runtime prerequisites (Node.js for `npx`, SQLite CLI availability) documented as assumptions and reflected in requirements? [Dependencies, Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-015]
- [X] CHK028 Is the dependency on `.env` + `env.example` alignment explicit and verifiable? [Dependencies, Spec §FR-002]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [X] CHK029 Is there any conflict between “normalized status model” and tool-specific status enumerations that needs disambiguation? [Conflict, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-008]
- [X] CHK030 Is a requirement-to-contract trace map defined so each checklist outcome maps to FR/SC clauses and contract sections? [Traceability, Gap]
